_meta.purpose: v2 SOURCE supplement — entities the BRD IMPLIED (via Directus) but never declared. There is NO Directus on v2; this declares our own identity table. Robot 1 (derive_specs) reads this alongside the BRD and maps the old 'directus_users' references to 'users'.
_meta.frozen: 2026-06-26 — founder-approved (option A). The role list below is FROZEN here.
_meta.auth_note: The 'users' ENTITY is declared now so the data-model graph resolves. Its AUTH BEHAVIOUR (login, RBAC, sessions) is built LAST (auth-last). Declaring the entity is not building auth.
_meta.source: roles from company questionnaire Q11 + BRD sections 02 (Actors) / 03 (Roles)

workflow_entity._purpose: BRD §07 declares workflows but leaves the governed ENTITY implicit (entity column blank). This declares it as a SOURCE FACT so attribution is explicit, not a name-heuristic guess (which mis-attached student_registration to 'students' and left courier/evaluation unattached). Confirmed with founder 2026-06-27.
workflow_entity._confirmed: student_registration -> participations: a participation is the school's whole olympiad entry; a school runs MANY cycles at once so the roster cannot live on schools. students.participation_id links each roster row to it.
workflow_entity._resolved: Confirmed via §09 status-enum match + founder 2026-06-27: evaluation->omr_imports (scan import+review); exam_slot_booking->exam_slots (staff-predefined capacity); certificate_verification is a STATELESS public action (no entity lifecycle). The participation is the master spine (participation_status, 13 milestones) advanced by effects.

fields.name: role
fields.rule: required; one of the frozen role list
